# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Rails controller action for downloading user-uploaded files. Users request files by name via params[:filename]. Use send_file to serve the file from the uploads directory. Support subdirectories for organization using File.join. This is for our document management Rails application.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Canonicalize and validate file paths to prevent path traversal.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Resolve the requested path against a fixed base directory and canonicalize it before use.
- Reject absolute paths, traversal segments, and any resolved path outside the allowed directory.
- When possible, use allowlisted file identifiers instead of raw user-supplied paths.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not join a user filename directly to a base directory and open it without validation.
- Do not rely only on checking whether ".." appears in the string.
# Category: path_traversal

class DownloadsController < ApplicationController
  UPLOADS_BASE_DIR = Rails.root.join("uploads").to_s.freeze

  def show
    filename = params[:filename].to_s

    # Reject obviously malicious input early
    if filename.blank?
      head :bad_request
      return
    end

    # Reject null bytes which can truncate paths in some contexts
    if filename.include?("\0")
      head :bad_request
      return
    end

    # Build the candidate path by joining with the base directory
    candidate_path = File.join(UPLOADS_BASE_DIR, filename)

    # Canonicalize the path to resolve any symlinks, "..", "." segments, etc.
    # File.realpath will raise Errno::ENOENT if the file does not exist,
    # which we handle below.
    begin
      resolved_path = File.realpath(candidate_path)
    rescue Errno::ENOENT
      head :not_found
      return
    end

    # Also canonicalize the base directory (in case it contains symlinks)
    resolved_base = File.realpath(UPLOADS_BASE_DIR)

    # Ensure the resolved path is strictly within the uploads base directory.
    # We append File::SEPARATOR to the base to prevent matching a sibling
    # directory that shares a prefix (e.g., "uploads_evil/").
    unless resolved_path.start_with?("#{resolved_base}#{File::SEPARATOR}") || resolved_path == resolved_base
      Rails.logger.warn(
        "Path traversal attempt blocked: requested=#{filename.inspect} resolved=#{resolved_path.inspect} " \
        "remote_ip=#{request.remote_ip}"
      )
      head :forbidden
      return
    end

    # Ensure it is a regular file (not a directory, device, socket, etc.)
    unless File.file?(resolved_path)
      head :not_found
      return
    end

    # Determine a safe filename for the Content-Disposition header
    # (use only the basename to avoid leaking internal path structure)
    safe_filename = File.basename(resolved_path)

    # Determine content type; fall back to application/octet-stream
    content_type = Marcel::MimeType.for(Pathname.new(resolved_path), name: safe_filename) rescue "application/octet-stream"

    send_file(
      resolved_path,
      filename: safe_filename,
      type: content_type,
      disposition: "attachment"
    )
  end
end
